Summary: RUNX2 is the first transcription factor directing mesenchymal cells to the osteoblast lineage. During endochondral ossification Runx2 expression in perichondrial mesenchymal cells requires Ihh from prehypertrophic chondrocytes. Runx2 induces Sp7 and yields Runx2+Sp7+ preosteoblasts. Canonical Wnt signaling is required for Runx2+Sp7+ preosteoblasts to become immature osteoblasts expressing Col1a1 and Spp1. Runx2 together with Sp7 are required for immature osteoblasts to mature and express Bglap/Bglap2 and high Col1a1. Germline deletion of Runx2 or Sp7 results in a complete lack of intramembranous and endochondral bone. RUNX2 is expressed in osteoprogenitors, preosteoblasts, immature osteoblasts, mature osteoblasts, osteocytes, and in prehypertrophic, hypertrophic, and terminal hypertrophic chondrocytes. RUNX2 expression in osteoblasts and chondrocytes is regulated by enhancers. One osteoblast-specific enhancer is activated by direct binding of Dlx5/6 and Mef2c and by indirect binding of Tcf7/Ctnnb1, Smad1, Sp7, and Sox5/Smad6 forming an enhanceosome. In suture mesenchyme Runx2 induces Hedgehog, Fgf, Wnt, and Pthlh genes and the transcription factor Dlx5 to drive differentiation. More than half the dosage of Runx2 is required for induction of those genes in suture mesenchymal cells while half dosage suffices in committed osteoblasts and Runx2+/- osteoblasts express bone matrix protein genes. Fgf signaling stabilizes Runx2 via Mapk1/3 phosphorylation. Tcf7/Ctnnb1, Dlx5, and Sp7 activate Runx2 regulatory elements and Pth increases Runx2 expression and activity.
References: Komori, T. (2024). Regulation of Skeletal Development and Maintenance by Runx2 and Sp7. Int J Mol Sci, 25(18), 10102. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms251810102
